## Summary
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Panel ID** | [object Object] |
| **Defect** | Dusty |
| **Confidence** | 93.0% |
| **Urgency** | 🟡 MEDIUM - Schedule cleaning within 1-2 weeks |

## Maintenance Actions
*   Schedule panel cleaning within the next 1-2 weeks.
*   Visually inspect the panel for dust accumulation and any other obvious defects prior to cleaning.
*   Clean the panel to remove all dust buildup, following standard cleaning constraints.
*   Avoid working near live conductors during the cleaning process.
*   Record panel_id, timestamp, predicted defect, confidence, technician notes, and actions taken.

## Safety
*   Always adhere to site-specific safety procedures.
*   Wear appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE).
*   Avoid working near live conductors.
*   Ensure stable footing and fall protection when working at heights.

## Materials / Tools
*   Soft brush or non-abrasive sponge.
*   Clean water (e.g., de-ionized).
*   Mild, non-abrasive cleaning solution (if needed).
*   Squeegee or lint-free cloth.
*   Appropriate access equipment (e.g., ladder, boom lift).
*   PPE (e.g., gloves, safety glasses).

## Verification
*   Visually confirm the panel surface is free of dust and debris.
*   Inspect the panel for any new scratches or damage after cleaning.
*   If performance remains suboptimal, consider module-level electrical measurements (V, I) using a multimeter/clamp meter.
*   Record module ID, V_oc, I_sc, irradiance, and temperature if performing electrical checks.
*   Monitor system performance data to confirm improved energy output post-cleaning.
